name: Sleep Sachet
description: A gentle sachet for restful sleep and vivid, protected dreams. Place under the pillow or hang near the bed.
steps: Grind or crumble the dried herbs lightly to release their scent.
Combine all herbs in the muslin bag.
Hold the sachet between your palms and visualize deep, unbroken sleep. Set your intention clearly.
Place under your pillow before bed.
notes: Replace monthly or when the scent fades. Mugwort intensifies dreaming — reduce or omit if dreams become too vivid.

name: Protection Jar
description: A defensive jar that creates a barrier against ill intent, hexes, and unwanted spiritual intrusion. Seal with black wax and place near your front entrance.
steps: Layer the salt into the bottom of the jar to create a cleansing base.
Add the herbs one at a time, focusing on their protective properties as you place each one.
Seal the jar tightly.
Drip black wax over the lid to seal your intention.
Hold the jar and state your protective intention clearly aloud or in your mind.
Place near your front door, on a windowsill, or bury near the property boundary.
notes: Shake the jar when you feel your home needs a boost. Replace yearly or if the jar breaks.

name: Money Drawing Sachet
description: A classic prosperity sachet carried in the wallet or purse to attract financial opportunity and draw money toward you.
steps: On a Thursday (Jupiter's day) or during a waxing or full moon, combine the herbs.
Hold the lodestone or pyrite in your hands and charge it with your intention to attract abundance.
Place all ingredients in the green bag.
Tie the bag closed with three knots, sealing your intention with each knot.
Carry in your wallet, purse, or cash drawer.
notes: Feed the sachet with a drop of basil essential oil or fresh mint once a month to keep it active.

name: Dream Pillow
description: A pillow insert or sachet stuffed with herbs that encourage vivid, prophetic, and lucid dreams. Especially powerful at the new and full moon.

ingredients.item: Small cloth pillowcase or large sachet

steps: Combine all herbs and fill the pillow or sachet loosely (don't pack too tight).
Sew or tie shut.
Place inside your pillowcase beneath your regular pillow.
Before sleep, press the pillow to your face and breathe in the scent while stating your intention to remember your dreams.
notes: Keep a journal by the bed. Mugwort can be potent — reduce the amount if dreams become overwhelming or disturbing.

name: Purification Bath
description: A spiritual cleansing bath to wash away negativity, psychic residue, spiritual contamination, or the aftermath of intense magical work.

ingredients.item: Sea salt or pink Himalayan salt

steps: Place all dried herbs in the muslin bag.
Run a warm bath and hang or float the herb bag in the water while the bath fills.
Add the salt to the bath and stir clockwise while visualizing all negativity dissolving.
Light the white candle.
Enter the bath and soak for at least 15 minutes, allowing the water to draw away what no longer serves you.
Drain the bath while still seated, visualizing the unwanted energy draining away with the water.
notes: Air dry if possible rather than toweling off immediately. Perform at the full or waning moon for releasing, or as needed after heavy magical work or difficult experiences.

name: Hex-Breaking Jar
description: A jar working to break hexes, remove crossed conditions, and return negative energy to its source. Uses the fierce, reversing energy of Mars-ruled plants.

ingredients.item: Small mirror shard or reflective piece

steps: Working on a waning moon, preferably on a Saturday (Saturn) or Tuesday (Mars), combine the herbs.
If using a mirror shard, place it in the jar with the reflective side facing out through the glass — to reflect workings back.
Fill the jar with the herbs and seal tightly.
Seal with black wax while stating: 'What has been sent returns. This working is broken.'
Bury the jar away from your property, or place it on your altar and shake it three times when you feel the crossed condition active.
notes: Wash hands thoroughly after handling wormwood and rue. Dispose of the jar away from your home once you feel the working has cleared.

name: Love Oil Blend
description: A Venus-ruled anointing oil for love work — worn on the body, used to dress candles, or added to sachets to draw romantic love and strengthen connection.

ingredients.item: Carrier oil (sweet almond, jojoba, or fractionated coconut)

steps: On a Friday (Venus's day) during a waxing moon, combine the dried herbs in the bottle.
Pour the carrier oil over them.
Add the essential oils.
Cap the bottle and roll it gently between your palms to blend, focusing on your intention.
Let the oil infuse for at least one full lunar cycle (28 days) before using.
Strain out the dried herbs before use if desired, or leave them in.
notes: Anoint pulse points (wrists, throat, heart) before going out or before love work. Use to dress a pink or red candle for attraction spells.

name: Ancestor Offering Blend
description: A loose incense blend burned on charcoal to honor ancestors, open communication with the beloved dead, and create sacred space for spirit work.

ingredients.amount: 1/2 tsp dried or a small braid

ingredients.item: A photo or memento of your ancestor (optional)

steps: Set up your ancestor altar or working space. Place photos, mementos, or offerings of food and water if you have them.
Light the charcoal disc and allow it to fully catch (it will spark and glow orange).
Combine the herbs and resins.
Place a small amount of the blend on the glowing charcoal.
As the smoke rises, speak to your ancestors — invite them to join you, share news, ask for guidance, or simply offer gratitude.
Allow the incense to burn through naturally.
notes: Burn in a well-ventilated space. Wormwood smoke can be potent — use small amounts. This blend can be used at Samhain, Días de los Muertos, or any time you wish to connect with the beloved dead.
